system_description.overview: BIChart is an enterprise SaaS platform that automates the migration of Tableau dashboards to Power BI, including visuals, data models, and DAX logic. The platform leverages AI to transform Tableau expressions into DAX queries and includes features to transform visuals from one BI service to another with no engineering lift.

products.Automated Business Intelligence Migratio: End-to-end automation for migrating Tableau dashboards, workbooks, data models, and calculated fields into Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric environments
products.AI-Powered Analytics Conversion: AI-assisted conversion of Tableau calculations and logic into DAX and Power BI-compatible structures with semantic understanding of visual layouts and intelligent error detection
products.Data and Workspace Management: Secure handling of customer metadata, report definitions, and transformation logic with workspace configuration aligned with Microsoft Fabric architecture and role-based access
products.Implementation and Support Services: White-glove onboarding and training for enterprise users with access to BIChart support specialists for migration assistance and validation

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; HQ address is 3452 Margaret St Miami FL, 33133 but no physical servers - all cloud

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: AWS Amplify (serves as application gateway per network diagram)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings. Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured to prevent unauthorized access and detect external security threats.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS encryption using certificates from established third-party certificate authority for all web-based system requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet through this system
network_architecture.topology: User -> Amazon Cognito (authentication) -> AWS Amplify -> Lambda Functions (retrieve/migrate data) -> AWS Secrets Manager (DB creds) -> PostgreSQL (store data) / AWS Fargate (task execution) -> S3 (file uploads) -> Google Cloud Run (LLM processing)

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments confirmed (CC8.1.4: changes developed and tested in separate environment before implementation)

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA enforced for all remote access. MFA required for source code management tool access. Two-factor authentication required for employee email access.
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.key_management: AWS KMS - cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest and store/manage encryption keys. Access to production access keys restricted to authorized individuals.

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (specific vendor not named; MFA-protected access, changes logged/timestamped/attributed to author)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not specifically named; formal change management policy with development lifecycle documented
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code confirmed - baseline configurations retained within IaC, configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews by CTO. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance. OS patches managed and applied as they become available.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not specifically named; monitoring tools used for continuous system capacity and performance monitoring with threshold-based alerting
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not specifically named; logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure provider - compute, storage, networking, identity, secrets management, serverless
LLM processing for AI-powered analytics conversion

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution with anti-virus, anti-malware, and Trojan protection installed on all desktops, laptops, and production servers. Automatic scanning with daily definition updates. Weekly anti-virus definition updates for production servers. Mac-based workstations also covered.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications with frequency adjusted as needed. Internal IT Infrastructure team performs periodic scans. Self-assessment scan prior to every production release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. High/critical findings remediated and tracked via risk register.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Significant changes to people/roles/responsibilities could not be tested because there were no significant changes during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance control could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Security incident response could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls excluded; AWS responsible for physical security of data centers
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; BIChart has no physical office/servers, managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually to review internal controls
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly to review security activities and performance metrics
Formal vendor management policy with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for vendors without SOC reports
Source code changes logged, timestamped, and attributed with MFA-protected access to source code tool
Baseline configurations retained as infrastructure-as-code with configuration management tool for rollback

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with multi-AZ, VPC segmentation, and IaC for baseline configs. However, single region (no multi-region), no CDN mentioned, and relies on secondary cloud (Google Cloud Run) without clear documentation of that integration. Serverless-heavy architecture with Lambda and Fargate is modern.

application_architecture.rationale: Serverless-heavy pattern with Lambda, Fargate, and Cloud Run is modern but no languages or frameworks disclosed. Branch protection and code review are confirmed. Separate environments exist. No mention of testing frameworks, API style, or detailed architecture beyond the network diagram.

data_layer.rationale: Single database type (PostgreSQL) with S3 for file storage. Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies exist. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. However, no caching layer, no data warehouse, and no specifics on backup retention periods or replication strategy.

security_posture.rationale: Strong access controls with MFA, RBAC, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation SLA. IDS/IPS, firewalls, endpoint protection, annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scanning, server hardening, and cybersecurity insurance all present. Encryption at rest (KMS) and in transit (TLS). No WAF or SIEM mentioned. No specific TLS version disclosed.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed with configuration management tool and rollback capability. Branch protection with required approval for production merges. Formal change management with emergency change process. Source code management with MFA. However, specific CI/CD tool and source control platform not named, and no mention of automated testing in pipeline.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual DR testing with different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover with daily backups. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, and DR details are in Section 5 (not audited). Procedures for spinning up across all AZs are described but not quantified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy AWS dependency for nearly all infrastructure (compute, storage, networking, identity, secrets). Google Cloud Run used only for LLM processing. Very few named vendors. High single-vendor concentration risk on AWS with carve-out model meaning their controls are not audited within this report.

overall.rationale: BIChart demonstrates a reasonably mature security posture for an early-stage enterprise SaaS company. Unqualified opinion with zero exceptions is positive. The serverless-heavy AWS architecture is modern but lacks multi-region redundancy and detailed technology disclosures. The very small team (5 people in org chart: CEO, CTO, CMO, Lead Engineer, Data Analyst Intern) raises concerns about key-person dependency and operational resilience. Many technology specifics (languages, frameworks, CI/CD tools, monitoring tools, SIEM) are not disclosed, limiting the depth of technical assessment.

red_flags: Extremely small team: only 5 people shown in org chart (CEO, CTO, CMO, Lead Engineer, Data Analyst Intern) - significant key-person dependency risk
No SIEM or named monitoring/alerting platform disclosed
No WAF mentioned despite being a web application
Google Cloud Run used for LLM processing creates multi-cloud complexity without clear documentation of security controls spanning both clouds
Processing Integrity trust criteria excluded despite being an AI-powered data transformation platform where processing accuracy is core to the product
Privacy trust criteria excluded despite handling customer Tableau metadata and report definitions
3-month audit period (May-Aug 2025) is relatively short for a Type 2 report
yellow_flags: No specific TLS version mentioned (could be using older TLS)
No CDN mentioned for a web application
Many security tools referenced generically without naming specific products (monitoring tool, endpoint security solution, source code management tool)
4 controls could not be tested for operating effectiveness due to no events occurring during the engagement period
Section 5 (DR details) explicitly not audited by independent auditor
No mention of SSO for enterprise customers despite targeting enterprise market
Audit firm (Accorp Partners, PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383) is not a well-known Big 4 or major audit firm
green_flags: Unqualified opinion with zero exceptions on all tested controls
MFA enforced across production access, remote access, source code, and email
Infrastructure as code with configuration management and rollback capability
Branch protection with required approval for production merges - developers cannot push directly to production
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and backup failure alerting
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
Quarterly user access reviews including administrative accounts
1 business day access revocation SLA upon termination
Formal vendor management program with annual SOC report reviews
Server hardening with documented baseline configurations
key_observations: The network diagram reveals a hybrid cloud architecture: AWS for core infrastructure but Google Cloud Run for LLM processing - this multi-cloud dependency is not discussed in the system description text
Amazon Cognito is used for authentication (visible in network diagram) but not mentioned by name in the text - suggests the report was not thoroughly cross-referenced between diagram and narrative
AWS Secrets Manager is used for database credential management (per diagram) - a strong practice but not highlighted in the control descriptions
The org chart shows only 5 people total, with a Data Analyst Intern - this is an extremely lean team for an enterprise SaaS company undergoing SOC 2 certification
AWS Amplify serves as the application layer (per diagram), suggesting a relatively simple frontend architecture
Despite being an AI company, there is no mention of AI/ML-specific security controls, model governance, or data handling procedures for the LLM component
The report excludes both Processing Integrity and Privacy criteria, which is unusual for a data transformation platform that handles customer business intelligence data
No specific regions or availability zones are named despite multi-AZ being confirmed - prevents assessment of data residency compliance
